column2: Collapse 4 individual PR-validation workflows into one orchestrator (gates.yml) that chains expensive gates behind a free smoke job
column3: Previous architecture fired all four validators in parallel on every PR push (canon-code-sync Managed Agent gate, canon-code-sync runtime probe, oddkit-gauntlet runtime probe, output-artifact-validation runtime probe). Each runtime probe costs $0.05-0.50 in Anthropic API charges; the legacy Managed Agent gate is similar. With Bugbot auto-iterating fixes, every push fires the whole set — a typical session burns $5-20 in CI agent costs alone before any operator review. New architecture: one orchestrator workflow with a single smoke job (typecheck + YAML frontmatter parse + TSV shape check, all free, ~60-90s total) that gates all four expensive jobs via `needs: [smoke]`. Per-job `if:` conditions also check `draft == false` and path-class membership. Smoke failure or draft state or missing path scope = expensive job skipped at zero Claude cost. Cancel-in-progress via concurrency group ensures Bugbot-driven iteration only pays for the most recent commit's verdict, not every intermediate one.

column2: Smoke runs typecheck, YAML frontmatter validation, and Dolcheo+ TSV shape validation; URI integrity stays at the probe layer
column3: Smoke contents (all free, all conditional on relevant paths changed): (1) tsc --noEmit on worker/ if worker code changed, (2) python3 .github/scripts/validate-frontmatter.py on canon/**/*.md and writings/**/*.md changes (parse YAML, assert uri+title+status present, assert URI scheme is ams:// or klappy://), (3) python3 .github/scripts/validate-tsv-shape.py on journal/**/*.tsv changes (≥3 fields per row, first field is single Dolcheo letter D/O/L/C/H/E; tolerates a legacy header row at line 1 so PRs that touch old journals don't fail at smoke for unrelated reasons). URI integrity audit was considered but deferred — oddkit_audit lives over MCP, and a curl-friendly equivalent would require its own session bootstrap. The runtime probes already include URI checking in their Claude-driven task; smoke catches the cheap mechanical issues and lets the probes handle deeper drift.

column2: Path classification in smoke emits 4 boolean outputs that downstream jobs consume via `needs.smoke.outputs.X`
column3: Smoke computes `git diff --name-only base..head`, then classifies the result against four path classes: canon_or_writings_or_journal (Oddkit Gauntlet scope), journal_tsv (Output-Artifact scope), legacy_gate_paths (the canon-code-sync legacy gate + runtime probe parity-twin scope, mirrors the old canon-code-sync-audit.yml `paths:` filter), worker_code (triggers TypeScript typecheck in smoke itself). Each downstream gate gates on the right class. Output-Artifact and Canon-Code Sync runtime probe previously had no `paths:` filter at all and fired on every PR regardless of relevance — that wasted ~30% of Claude calls before chaining even kicked in.

column2: The legacy Managed Agent gate moved into gates.yml as a normal job, not a reusable workflow
column3: The legacy gate's steps (checkout, dispatch python script, post PR comment, exit on verdict) are inlined into gates.yml under the canon-code-sync-legacy job. The alternative was extracting them into a separate reusable workflow and calling them from gates.yml the way the runtime probes call audit-gate-runtime-probe.yml. Inlining is simpler — fewer files, less ceremony — and the legacy gate's steps don't have other callers. If the legacy path needs to be called from elsewhere later, refactor then.

column2: The four replaced workflow files are deleted in this PR — pull_request triggers only fire from existing files
column3: canon-code-sync-audit.yml, canon-code-sync-audit-runtime-probe.yml, oddkit-gauntlet-runtime-probe.yml, and output-artifact-validation-runtime-probe.yml are removed in this PR. Once merged, gates.yml is the only entry point for these checks. The reusable workflow audit-gate-runtime-probe.yml is preserved unchanged — gates.yml's three probe jobs call it with different persona_uri values, same as the deleted callers did. Status check names change: `Canon-Code Sync Audit (Managed Agent)` becomes `Gates / Canon-Code Sync Audit (Managed Agent)` and similarly for the others. If any branch protection rules reference the old names, they need to be updated post-merge.

column2: This PR cannot exercise the new orchestrator on itself — validation gap disclosure per canon
column3: Per ams://canon/constraints/validators-cannot-self-validate-their-own-fix-prs, this PR modifies a production-bound validator (the gate workflows themselves) and cannot exercise the new orchestrator on its own gates. The OLD workflows are what runs on this PR — they fire as 4 parallel checks against this PR's diff. The new gates.yml file is on the PR branch but is not yet active. The validation event is the first PR opened AFTER this merges: that PR triggers gates.yml as the live orchestrator. Expected behavior on that validation event: (1) smoke job fires on every push including drafts; (2) probe jobs skip when the PR is in draft; (3) probe jobs skip when their per-class path filter has no match; (4) probe jobs only fire when smoke passes AND the PR is out of draft AND the path class is present; (5) cancel-in-progress halts in-flight Claude calls when new commits land. If smoke incorrectly skips checks or incorrectly fails on clean files, the validation event is also where that surfaces.

column2: Smoke validators tested locally against existing repo files
column3: validate-frontmatter.py was tested against 5 existing canon files (canon-code-sync-via-spawned-agent-session, mcp-build-side-governance, mcp-wrapper-conformance-for-conversational-ai, ams-canon-code-auditor, ams-oddkit-gauntlet-runner) — all passed. validate-tsv-shape.py was tested against the old journal style (with header row, 2026-05-01-ams-d0009) and the new style (no header, 2026-05-13 entries) — both pass after the header-tolerance heuristic was added. Pyyaml is required for the frontmatter validator; the smoke job pip-installs it conditionally before running.

column2: Post-merge: first PR opened after this merges is the orchestrator validation event
column3: On merge, gates.yml becomes the live PR validation workflow. The deleted files no longer trigger anything (they don't exist on main). Any PR opened after this should show the new shape: a "Smoke" check appearing on every push, and the expensive probes appearing only when their conditions are met. Branch protection rules (if any) that referenced the old workflow file names need to be updated to reference gates.yml's job names. Worth a one-line followup if needed: check repo settings -> branches -> protection rules for stale required-check entries. Standing flags unchanged: PAT and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in project instructions remain unrotated.
